---
title: "Nodes and Edges"
format: html
previous:
  href: "intro-what_is_a_network.qmd"
  text: "What is a Network"

next:
  href: "intro-dyad.qmd"
  text: "The Dyad"
---

### Nodes

Nodes (or “actors”) are the operational units studied in a network. In our area of interest these might be research groups, institutes, enterprise units, Library / professional services, communities.

::: callout-note
In our dataset each row = one node
:::

### Edges

Edges (or “ties”) are the connected relationships between nodes. Edges might represent collaboration, engagement, shared people.

::: callout-note
In our dataset edges will be created from lists of nodes which have a relationship with the node represented by each row.
:::

## Example

#### example data

| Label  | Node | Relationships |
|--------|------|---------------|
| Node 1 | N1   | N2            |
| Node 2 | N2   | N1; N3        |
| Node 3 | N3   | N2            |

#### example network

```{r echo=FALSE, fig.height=2}

library(igraph)
library(ggraph)
library(tidygraph)

# Define edges directly (clean and explicit)
edges <- data.frame(
  from = c("N1", "N2"),
  to   = c("N2", "N3")
)

# Define nodes with labels
nodes <- data.frame(
  name = c("N1", "N2", "N3"),
  label = c("Node 1", "Node 2", "Node 3")
)

# Build graph
g <- graph_from_data_frame(edges, vertices = nodes, directed = FALSE)
graph <- as_tbl_graph(g)

ggraph(graph, layout = "linear") +
  geom_edge_link(aes(label = "Edge"), angle_calc = "along", label_dodge = unit(2, "mm")) +
  geom_node_point(size = 6) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = label),vjust = -1.5, size = 4) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")+
  theme_void()+
  theme(
    
    plot.background = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = "white", linewidth = 0.5),
    plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)

  )
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: nav-footer
```{=html}
<a href="{{< meta previous.href >}}" aria-label="Go to previous page: {{< meta previous.text >}}">
  <span aria-hidden="true">«</span> {{< meta previous.text >}}
</a>

<a href="{{< meta next.href >}}" aria-label="Go to next page: {{< meta next.text >}}">
  {{< meta next.text >}} <span aria-hidden="true">»</span>
</a>
```
:::
